title: Physically finishable but legally pressured
summary: Distinguish cars that can finish on current tyres from cars that can finish physically but still owe a compound or stop obligation.
rationale: The run suggested rule-state features are most meaningful when tied to finish feasibility. A car that can physically reach the flag may still have a regulatory or strategic reason to pit.
implementation_hint: Combine current-tyre can-finish, remaining dry-compound debt after the current row, observed stop debt if available, and race phase. Add flags and margins for can-finish-but-owes-dry-compound, can-finish-but-owes-stop, and late-race legal pressure.
expected_effect: Small to medium ROC AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Regulation proxies are approximate and can be wrong for wet races, red flags, or special race rules; include exemption flags and avoid over-specific assumptions.
